Do you know when the meeting starts?

I was wondering if you could clarify that.
Could you tell me where the room is?
Good morning. Welcome to our meeting. Could you tell me your name, please?
Good morning. I am Doctor Carter, a guest speaker from Toronto.
Thank you. Do you know how many minutes you need?
About ten minutes. Would you mind sharing your screen?
Not at all. I was wondering if you could clarify one point.
Of course. Which point would you like me to clarify?
Could you tell me the title of your talk?
Yes. The title is Safety in the ICU.
Perfect. Would you mind if we record the meeting?
No problem. Please go ahead.
Do you know how many minutes you need?
Would you mind sharing your screen?
Would you mind if we record the meeting?

Hi Graziele, this is Doctor Carter. Before the meeting, a small favor. When I join, please welcome me and ask a few polite questions. You can say: Could you tell me your name? Do you know how many minutes you need? Would you mind sharing your screen? Keep it calm and kind. You will do great.

Hi Graziele, it is Ana. A quick tip about polite questions. To sound polite, start with Could you, Do you know, or Would you mind. Then keep normal word order: Could you tell me where the room is? Not where is the room. Softer words make a calmer meeting. Keep it simple. You will do great.

Graziele welcomes a guest speaker. First, the greeting: good morning and welcome. Then, the name: could you tell me your name, please? Next, the time: do you know how many minutes you need? After that, the screen: would you mind sharing your screen? Finally, the recording: would you mind if we record the meeting?
